rawCommits.subject: feat(analytics): add Mixpanel product analytics core setup
rawCommits.body:
Anonymous by default, no PII. Tracks app_opened, user_signed_up,
user_logged_in, and chat_message_sent with model property.
Respects DNT/GPC browser signals. Disabled when VITE_MIXPANEL_TOKEN
is unset. Opt-out support via localStorage.
rawCommits.cleanSubject: add Mixpanel product analytics core setup

rawCommits.subject: feat(analytics): add subscription/purchase tracking and privacy opt-out
rawCommits.body:
Track subscription_started with plan, credits_purchased with amount.
Add Privacy section in Settings with opt-in/opt-out toggle for
anonymous usage data sharing.
rawCommits.cleanSubject: add subscription/purchase tracking and privacy opt-out

rawCommits.subject: feat(analytics): track all major product events
rawCommits.body:
Add 16 Mixpanel events covering the full product surface: project
create/open, agent create, task create/run, process create/trigger,
AURA 3D image/model generation, feedback, marketplace hire,
integration connect, note create, settings/tier modal open, and
invite code copy.

rawCommits.subject: feat(analytics): track model selection, agent selection, file attachment, and session reset
rawCommits.body:
Complete the remaining plan events: model_selected with model_name,
agent_selected (guarded against null clears), file_attached with
file_count, and chat_session_reset across all three reset paths.
rawCommits.cleanSubject: track model selection, agent selection, file attachment, and session reset

rawCommits.subject: fix(server): capture dev-loop run bundles

rawCommits.subject: feat(server): tier 1 fail-fast preflight for harness session identity
rawCommits.body:
Add a server-side preflight that fails loudly with HTTP 422
`session_identity_missing` when a chat / dev-loop / project-tool
session would be opened against the harness with one of the required
`X-Aura-*` identity fields missing (org id, session id, template /
agent id, user id, JWT). Without this, the harness silently dropped
the missing header and the first LLM call surfaced later as a
Cloudflare 403 / generic 5xx with no actionable signal.

* `ApiError::session_identity_missing(field, context)` constructor
  emits a stable `missing_<field>` code plus structured
  `data.context` so the UI / evals pipeline / integration tests can
  match without parsing free text.
* `handlers::agents::session_identity` ships per-call-site
  `SessionIdentityRequirements` (CHAT, DEV_LOOP, PROJECT_TOOL,
  GENERATION, SCHEDULED_PROCESS) plus `validate_session_identity` /
  `validate_automaton_identity` helpers. PROJECT_TOOL relaxes
  `template_agent_id` to "any agent identity" so the synthetic
  `{tool_agent_name}-{project_id}` agent_id used for project-tool
  sessions without a bound agent instance still passes.
* Wire the validators into `chat::open_harness_chat_stream`,
  `dev_loop::start_or_adopt`, `dev_loop::run_single_task`, and
  `project_tool_session_config` so every session-open call site is
  guarded uniformly.

Side-effect cleanup: drop the three Windows-only
`debug_log_session_shape*` shims that landed in 4cffca5f1 and wrote
synchronously to a hardcoded `C:\code\aura-os\debug-95fd5c.log`
on every chat / dev-loop / project-tool start (silently dropped on
Linux/macOS, unbounded growth, leaked data-length metrics). Replaced
with a single structured `tracing::debug!` event on the
`aura_os_server::session_shape` target gated by normal `RUST_LOG`
controls.

Co-authored-by: Cursor <cursoragent@cursor.com>
rawCommits.cleanSubject: tier 1 fail-fast preflight for harness session identity

rawCommits.subject: feat(harness): tier 2 fail-fast preflight for session identity
rawCommits.body:
Add a harness-side identity preflight that mirrors the server's
Tier 1 contract so drift between the two layers stays observable
from either side. If a server build forgets to call Tier 1 (or a
direct caller bypasses it entirely), the harness now refuses to
emit a `SessionInit` / `POST /automaton/start` payload missing one
of the required `X-Aura-*` header sources instead of letting the
upstream proxy 403 without context.

* `HarnessError::SessionIdentityMissing { field, context }` carries
  the canonical wire field name (`aura_org_id`, `aura_session_id`,
  `agent_id`, `auth_token`) and a static call-site label
  (`session_init` / `automaton_start`).
* New `HarnessError::session_identity_missing(&anyhow::Error)`
  walks the cause chain to extract the structured variant — same
  pattern the existing `is_capacity_exhausted` helper uses.
* `validate_session_init_identity(&SessionConfig)` and
  `validate_automaton_start_identity(&AutomatonStartParams)`
  enforce the *intersection* of fields every real call site needs;
  `user_id` is intentionally not required at this layer because
  ad-hoc CLI / scheduled-process flows in `runner.rs` /
  `process_automaton.rs` legitimately omit it.
* Wire the validators into `LocalHarness::open_session`,
  `SwarmHarness::open_session`, and `AutomatonClient::start` at the
  very top of each function so no network work happens before the
  preflight.
* Server's `map_harness_error_to_api` gains a new branch that
  funnels `HarnessError::SessionIdentityMissing` into
  `ApiError::session_identity_missing(field, context)` so callers
  see the same 422 response shape regardless of which side caught
  the missing field.

Adds 18 new unit tests across the harness validators, error helper,
and the server's mapping branch.

Co-authored-by: Cursor <cursoragent@cursor.com>
rawCommits.cleanSubject: tier 2 fail-fast preflight for session identity

rawCommits.subject: feat(server): give bare-agent chat parity with instance route
rawCommits.body:
Bring the `POST /v1/agents/:agent_id/chat/stream` route in line with
the project-bound instance route when an effective `project_id` is
present (either via the explicit `body.project_id` or inferred from
the agent's `find_matching_project_agents` binding). The bare-agent
route used to ship the raw template `system_prompt` and an empty
`SessionConfig::project_path`, which silently dropped workspace
context: the LLM never saw the canonical `<project_context>` block,
and the harness's filesystem / command tools resolved against an
empty cwd instead of the real repo. This was visible on the CEO
agent's `send_to_agent` flow and on any direct bare-agent chat
that targeted a project.

The new `build_agent_system_prompt` helper:

* Resolves `project_path` via `resolve_project_tool_workspace_path`
  with `agent_instance_id = None` so the bare-agent path falls
  through to the project-level workspace resolution (handles both
  explicit `local_workspace_path` and the canonical `data_dir`
  layout for Local / Swarm).
* Wraps the agent template prompt with `build_project_system_prompt`
  so the LLM gets the same `<project_context>` + IMPORTANT
  reminders the instance route emits.
* Falls back to the bare template prompt with no workspace path
  when there is no effective project, preserving the legacy
  unbound-bare-agent semantics.
* Appends the project-state snapshot last, matching the instance
  route's order.

Also threads the *effective* project id (rather than just
`body.project_id`) into `SessionConfig.project_id` so a bare-agent
chat that inferred its project from the persistence binding now
hands the harness a consistent `(project_id, project_path,
system_prompt)` triple — preventing the Tier 1 preflight from
warning on a project_id-without-project_path mismatch.

Co-authored-by: Cursor <cursoragent@cursor.com>
rawCommits.cleanSubject: give bare-agent chat parity with instance route

rawCommits.subject: feat(server,harness): map dev-loop WS connect capacity to 503
rawCommits.body:
Surface upstream WS-slot exhaustion that occurs during the
*event-stream* connect (not just the initial `/automaton/start`
HTTP call) as the structured `harness_capacity_exhausted` 503
envelope instead of a generic `bad_gateway` 502.

Previously, when a dev-loop / single-task automaton successfully
posted to `/automaton/start` but the subsequent WS upgrade for
`/automaton/{id}/stream` was rejected with HTTP 503 / WS 1013
"Try Again Later", the dev-loop adapter mapped the error to
`ApiError::bad_gateway(...)` with a flattened message. The frontend
had no way to recognise the capacity case from the wire shape and
the eval pipeline's retry/backoff logic that keys on the structured
`harness_capacity_exhausted` code never fired.

Three coordinated changes:

* `AutomatonClient::connect_event_stream` now wraps WS-1013 / 503
  errors with `HarnessError::CapacityExhausted` using the same
  detection as `LocalHarness::open_session`
  (`is_capacity_exhausted_ws_error`), which is now `pub(crate)`.
* `runner::connect_with_retries` returns `anyhow::Result<...>`
  instead of `Result<_, String>` so the typed cause survives into
  the dev-loop adapter, AND short-circuits on a detected capacity
  rejection (no point burning two more retry attempts at 500ms /
  1000ms intervals when the upstream WS-slot semaphore won't free
  up that quickly).
* `dev_loop::adapter::start_loop` and `run_single_task` route the
  `connect_with_retries` error through the shared
  `map_harness_error_to_api` so the 503 envelope wins over the
  generic fallback. The fallback closure is preserved for any
  non-capacity transport failure.

`runner::start_and_connect` keeps its existing `RunStartError::Connect
{ message: String }` variant for backwards compatibility with the
ad-hoc CLI runner; it now stringifies the typed error at the
boundary instead of receiving a pre-stringified one.

Co-authored-by: Cursor <cursoragent@cursor.com>
rawCommits.cleanSubject: map dev-loop WS connect capacity to 503

rawCommits.subject: feat(server,harness): thread session identity through generation + scheduled processes
rawCommits.body:
Bring the two adjacent harness call sites — image / 3D generation
streams and scheduled-process automatons — onto the same identity
contract chat / dev-loop already meet, so every outbound proxy
`/v1/messages` call carries the canonical `X-Aura-Org-Id` /
`X-Aura-Session-Id` / `X-Aura-User-Id` headers. Without this, eval
bursts that exercised these surfaces bucketed as anonymous
IP-only traffic on aura-router and tripped the WAF rule chat
from the same account never reproduces.

Generation (`open_generation_stream`):

* New `GenerationIdentity { aura_org_id, aura_session_id, user_id }`
  bundle resolved by `resolve_generation_identity` from the auth
  session + (optional) project_id. When `project_id` is set the
  org is read from `project.org_id`; otherwise the user's first
  available org from `network_client.list_orgs(jwt)` is used as
  the fallback. Both surface a structured 422
  `session_identity_missing { field: "aura_org_id" }` if neither
  is available, instead of opening a session that would later
  trip the harness Tier 2 preflight.
* `aura_session_id` is a fresh UUID per stream — generation runs
  are stateless, unlike chat / dev-loop where the persisted
  session id is reused across reconnects.
* GENERATION `SessionIdentityRequirements` upgraded from "auth
  token only" to "org + session + user + auth token + any agent
  identity"; the synthetic `generation-{uuid}` agent_id now
  satisfies `require_any_agent_identity` in lieu of a
  `template_agent_id`.
* `harness.open_session` failures now route through
  `map_harness_error_to_api` so capacity exhaustion + harness-side
  identity preflight failures surface as the structured envelopes
  instead of a generic `bad_gateway`.

Scheduled processes (`process_automaton::start_scheduled_process_automaton`):

* `HarnessAutomatonStartParams` gains optional `aura_org_id` and
  `aura_session_id` fields, mirroring the same pattern already
  carried on `AutomatonStartParams` for dev-loop / single-task
  automata. Both serialize with `skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none"`
  so pre-existing harnesses (which `#[serde(default)]` the field)
  keep accepting the payload.
* `aura_org_id` is sourced from `process.org_id` when the storage
  record carries it, falling back to the locally-shadowed
  project's `org_id`.
* `aura_session_id` is a deterministic UUIDv5 derived from
  `(process_id, run_id)` so retries / restarts of the same
  logical run share a router bucket while concurrent runs of
  different processes get distinct bucketing — same rationale as
  `dev_loop::start::stable_dev_loop_session_id`.
* `map_automaton_start_error` now recognises the harness's WS-slot
  capacity 503 (empty body, opaque body, or canonical
  `code: "capacity_exhausted"`) and remaps to the structured
  `harness_capacity_exhausted` envelope instead of forwarding the
  raw 503 body as a generic `harness_error`.

Co-authored-by: Cursor <cursoragent@cursor.com>
rawCommits.cleanSubject: thread session identity through generation + scheduled processes

rendered.title: Product analytics, identity-safe sessions, and a polished slash-image flow
rendered.intro: Today's nightly lands a privacy-aware product analytics layer in the interface, hardens every harness session with a two-tier identity preflight on both server and harness sides, and finishes the slash-image generation experience end-to-end. Reliability work also reached the Windows updater handoff and the SWE-bench scoring pipeline.

entries.title: Mixpanel product analytics with built-in privacy controls
entries.summary: A new anonymous-by-default analytics layer landed across the interface, instrumenting key product events and giving users explicit privacy controls.

items.text: Introduced a Mixpanel-based analytics core that is anonymous by default, ships no PII, respects DNT/GPC browser signals, and stays disabled unless VITE_MIXPANEL_TOKEN is configured.

Instrumented the full product surface — projects, agents, tasks, processes, AURA 3D image and model generation, marketplace hires, integrations, notes, feedback, subscription starts and credit purchases, plus model selection, agent selection, file attachments and chat session resets.
Added a Privacy section in Org Settings with an opt-in/opt-out toggle for anonymous usage data, and tightened the panel layout so the row stays compact while context lives in a section intro.
Cleaned up TypeScript build breakage in the analytics platform detection and orbit project form so the new tracking compiles cleanly.

entries.title: Two-tier session identity preflight for every harness call site
entries.summary: Server- and harness-side preflights now fail loudly with a structured 422 the moment a session would open without its required X-Aura-* identity headers, replacing opaque downstream 403/5xx errors.

items.text: Added a Tier 1 server preflight that emits a stable session_identity_missing 422 with a per-field code whenever chat, dev-loop, project-tool, generation, or scheduled-process sessions are missing org id, session id, agent id, user id, or JWT — and removed the noisy Windows-only debug log shim that was writing synchronously to a hardcoded file on every session start.

Added a matching Tier 2 harness preflight on LocalHarness, SwarmHarness, and AutomatonClient::start so drift between server and harness stays observable from either side, with the server's error mapper funneling harness-side rejections into the same 422 response shape.

Threaded canonical org / session / user identity through image and 3D generation streams and scheduled-process automatons, with a deterministic UUIDv5 session id per (process_id, run_id) so retries share a router bucket while concurrent runs stay isolated.

Mapped dev-loop WebSocket connect failures (HTTP 503 / WS 1013) to the structured harness_capacity_exhausted 503 envelope and short-circuited retries, so the eval pipeline's capacity-aware backoff actually fires instead of seeing a generic bad_gateway.

Brought bare-agent chat to parity with the project-bound instance route by resolving the workspace path and wrapping the prompt with the canonical project_context block whenever an effective project is bound or inferred.
Wired harness stream events into loop-log bundles and live heuristics so dev-loop and SWE-bench failures retain actionable run evidence after the fact.

entries.title: Mixed clipboard pastes and a sturdier Windows updater handoff
entries.summary: Two targeted fixes improved chat input behavior and the Windows update experience.

items.text: Chat input now correctly accepts clipboard pastes that mix images with other content instead of dropping the image payload.
On Windows, the NSIS updater is now launched through PowerShell Start-Process so the installer survives Aura exiting mid-handoff, removing a class of stalled-update failures.

entries.title: SWE-bench scoring made explicit and recoverable
entries.summary: The SWE-bench aggregation pipeline now fails fast in unsupported configurations and reconciles misplaced harness reports.

items.text: Aggregation now errors out on native Windows unless driver-only mode is explicitly requested, and recovers misplaced AURA.<runId>.json harness reports so score.json reflects official results when they exist.

entries.title: Slash /image generation now works end-to-end in chat
entries.summary: A coordinated set of fixes routes the slash /image command through the generation stream, keeps it stable while queued, persists results, and renders them as proper media cards.

items.text: Slash /image now routes through the dedicated generation stream and preserves both image model and generation mode for queued sends, so the right backend and model are used even while another response is still streaming.
Completed the slash-image flow on both interface and server, normalizing the generation_completed payload shape and threading harness command channels through the SSE bridge.

Generated images are now finalized through the stream lifecycle into durable chat cards instead of being cleared with transient buffers, and render as standalone larger media rather than nested inside the generic tool block.

Stopped the live chat tail from rendering an optimistic prompt next to its persisted copy mid-stream, and stopped agent selection from forcing scroll and focus resets that yanked the chat pane around.

entries.title: Stratified SWE-bench smoke subset with richer postmortems
entries.summary: SWE-bench smoke runs now exercise a stratified subset and emit compact failure details to make environment, pollution, and hidden-test causes obvious from the postmortem alone.

items.text: Added a stratified SWE-bench smoke subset and embedded compact failure details in postmortems so benchmark runs surface environment, pollution, and hidden-test causes directly.

highlights: Mixpanel analytics shipped with DNT/GPC and an in-app opt-out
Two-tier session identity preflight prevents silent harness 403s
Slash /image generation now streams, persists, and renders as media
Windows updater handoff hardened via PowerShell Start-Process
